methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $400M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Sage Geosystems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $400M

private.name: Reverion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $310M

private.name: CarbonCapture Inc.

private.name: Cemvita
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
Pano AI is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Sonia Kastner that has raised $89M to date against a stated post-money valuation of $500M. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage private companies (Sage Geosystems at $400M, Reverion at $310M, CarbonCapture Inc. at $300M, and Cemvita at $120M), supplemented by one public comparable in the sector.

Two valuation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $500M last post-money valuation, implying a forward value of $950M. Peer-median analysis across the four Climate & Energy Series B comparables yields a median valuation of $305M, with a range of $120M to $400M. The peer cohort reflects meaningful dispersion, suggesting heterogeneous risk and growth profiles within the sector.

Triangulation of both methods produces a valuation range of $259M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $628M. The wide band reflects tension between the target's stated $500M+ valuation and the peer median of $305M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the four-company peer set, the applicability of the 1.9x multiplier to this specific company's trajectory, and the durability of Climate & Energy sector multiples in the current macro environment.
